return.speaker: narrator
return.text: The cup is gone from the table. So is the hood. He looks smaller without it, and older, and somehow more here than the corner has seen him be. A small bundle is on the chair beside him — wrapped cloth, a knife, a stone with a worn carving. A whole life, packed for a stranger.

quill-greet.speaker: quill
quill-greet.text: I am ready. I have been ready since the moment you said yes, but I needed the hours to believe it. Tell me only that you mean it. I will not be left behind in this corner a second time.

quill-promise.speaker: quill
quill-promise.text: I will keep watch the way the keepers used to keep watch — but for you, not for him. I know where his people set their snares. I know which stones do not strike back when struck. The careful eye of an old conspirator, turned around. That is what I have to offer.

choices.text: Walk with me. We leave the corner.
Hold a little longer. The road is not ready.

recruit.speaker: quill
recruit.text: ...Aye. Then we leave. The corner will be a corner again, without me in it. I would say something fitting. I cannot find the words. The drink took most of the words. The road will have to ask the rest of them out of me.

recruit-done.speaker: quill
recruit-done.text: One thing. If we pass a shrine of his — and we will, the road keeps several — let me walk past it without speaking. There are names of mine still tangled with that wood. I would rather they came home to me quietly, in their own time, than be called out by a stranger's curiosity.

decline.speaker: quill
decline.text: Of course. The corner has held me three winters; it can hold me a night more. I will not pack the bundle away. It has waited longer than that for a road. Find me when you are ready.
